sections.paragraph:
χηρεύω (χήρα) fut. 3 sg. χηρεύσει (SibOr 2, 206); aor. 3 sg. ἐχήρευσε Jer 28:5 (Hom., Od. 9, 124; Isaeus, Demosth., LXX; TestJud 12:1f; SibOr, Philo) lit. ‘be empty’, then be a widow τοὺς χηρεύοντας τοῦ λαοῦ GJs 8:3.—DELG s.v. χήρα.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

SibOr SibOr = Oracula Sibyllina, II–III a.d.—List 5

aor. aor. = aorist

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Od Od , s. Hom.; VIII? b.c.—List 5

Isaeus Isaeus , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJud TestJud = Testament of Judah, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1084
